title: Uninfected_Donor1_Untreated_Uninfected
treatment_protocol_ch1: Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Rv (MTB) was grown from a frozen stock to mid-log phase in 7H9 medium (Becton Dickinson), supplemented with albumin-dextrose-catalase (ADC, Becton Dickinson). Before infection, bacteria were washed three times and re-suspended in 1 ml PBS. Clumps were dissociated by 30 passages through a needle and then allowed to sediment for 5 min. Bacteria in the supernatant were then killed by heating at 90°C during 30min (defined heareafter hk-MTB). The density of bacteria in the supernatant was verified by measuring the OD600 and aliquot volumes defined to allow five bacterium-per two cell infection. Cells were infected in 6-well plates with each well containing 1.3 × 106 cells in 3 ml medium containing GM-CSF (R&D Systems), and treated with 20 µg/mL amikacin (AMK), 20 µg/mL ethambutol (EMB), 10 µg/mL isoniazid (INH), 200 µg/mL pyrazinamide (PZA), or 20 µg/mL rifampicin (RIF) for 18 h.
growth_protocol_ch1: Blood mononuclear cells from 4 healthy volunteers (Etablissement Français du Sang, Paris, France) were isolated by Ficoll-Paque (GE Healthcare Life Sciences) centrifugation. CD14+ monocytes were isolated by positive selection using CD14 microbeads (Miltenyi Biotec). Recovered cells were seeded at 2 × 106 cells/well in six-well plates (Falcon; BD Biosciences) in 3 ml RPMI 1640 (Invitrogen) supplemented with 10% heat-inactivated fetal calf serum (Dutscher), L-glutamine, GM-CSF (20 ng/ml; R&D Systems). Cultures were fed every 2 days with complete medium containing full doses of cytokine. Monocytes were allowed to differentiate into macrophages for 6 days at 37°C in 5% CO2 humidified atmosphere.
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted using QIAzol reagent (Life Technologies) and purified over RNeasy columns (Qiagen). The quality of all samples was assessed with an Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies) to verify RNA integrity. Only samples with a good RNA yield and no RNA degradation (ratio of 28S to 18S, > 1.7; RNA integrity number > 9) were used for further experiments.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: cDNA libraries were prepared with the Illumina TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit v2 and were sequenced on an llumina HiSeq 2500
data_processing: STAR v2.5.0b was used to map RNA-seq reads to the hg38 reference genome and quantify  gene expression (option--quantMode GeneCounts) by counting the fragments overlapping the Ensembl genes (GRCh38 v. 83).
data_processing.2: Supplementary_files_format_and_content: The raw counts were generated using STAR v2.5.0b for GRCh38 Ensembl genes

treatment_protocol_ch1: Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Rv (MTB) was grown from a frozen stock to mid-log phase in 7H9 medium (Becton Dickinson), supplemented with albumin-dextrose-catalase (ADC, Becton Dickinson). Before infection, bacteria were washed three times and re-suspended in 1 ml PBS. Clumps were dissociated by 30 passages through a needle and then allowed to sediment for 5 min. Bacteria in the supernatant were then killed by heating at 90°C during 30min (defined heareafter hk-MTB). The density of bacteria in the supernatant was verified by measuring the OD600 and aliquot volumes defined to allow five bacterium-per two cell infection. Cells were infected in 6-well plates with each well containing 1.3 × 106 cells in 3 ml medium containing GM-CSF (R&D Systems), and treated with 20 µg/mL amikacin (AMK), 20 µg/mL ethambutol (EMB), 10 µg/mL isoniazid (INH), 200 µg/mL pyrazinamide (PZA), or 20 µg/mL rifampicin (RIF) for 18 h.
growth_protocol_ch1: Blood mononuclear cells from 4 healthy volunteers (Etablissement Français du Sang, Paris, France) were isolated by Ficoll-Paque (GE Healthcare Life Sciences) centrifugation. CD14+ monocytes were isolated by positive selection using CD14 microbeads (Miltenyi Biotec). Recovered cells were seeded at 2 × 106 cells/well in six-well plates (Falcon; BD Biosciences) in 3 ml RPMI 1640 (Invitrogen) supplemented with 10% heat-inactivated fetal calf serum (Dutscher), L-glutamine, GM-CSF (20 ng/ml; R&D Systems). Cultures were fed every 2 days with complete medium containing full doses of cytokine. Monocytes were allowed to differentiate into macrophages for 6 days at 37°C in 5% CO2 humidified atmosphere.
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted using QIAzol reagent (Life Technologies) and purified over RNeasy columns (Qiagen). The quality of all samples was assessed with an Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies) to verify RNA integrity. Only samples with a good RNA yield and no RNA degradation (ratio of 28S to 18S, > 1.7; RNA integrity number > 9) were used for further experiments.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: cDNA libraries were prepared with the Illumina TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit v2 and were sequenced on an llumina HiSeq 2500
data_processing: STAR v2.5.0b was used to map RNA-seq reads to the hg38 reference genome and quantify  gene expression (option--quantMode GeneCounts) by counting the fragments overlapping the Ensembl genes (GRCh38 v. 83).
data_processing.2: Supplementary_files_format_and_content: The raw counts were generated using STAR v2.5.0b for GRCh38 Ensembl genes

Recovered cells were seeded at 2 × 106 cells/well in six-well plates (Falcon; BD Biosciences) in 3 ml RPMI 1640 (Invitrogen) supplemented with 10% heat-inactivated fetal calf serum (Dutscher), L-glutamine, GM-CSF (20 ng/ml; R&D Systems). Cultures were fed every 2 days with complete medium containing full doses of cytokine. Monocytes were allowed to differentiate into macrophages for 6 days at 37°C in 5% CO2 humidified atmosphere.
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted using QIAzol reagent (Life Technologies) and purified over RNeasy columns (Qiagen). The quality of all samples was assessed with an Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies) to verify RNA integrity. Only samples with a good RNA yield and no RNA degradation (ratio of 28S to 18S, > 1.7; RNA integrity number > 9) were used for further experiments.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: cDNA libraries were prepared with the Illumina TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit v2 and were sequenced on an llumina HiSeq 2500
data_processing: STAR v2.5.0b was used to map RNA-seq reads to the hg38 reference genome and quantify  gene expression (option--quantMode GeneCounts) by counting the fragments overlapping the Ensembl genes (GRCh38 v. 83).
data_processing.2: Supplementary_files_format_and_content: The raw counts were generated using STAR v2.5.0b for GRCh38 Ensembl genes

title: Infected_Donor3_Untreated_Infected
treatment_protocol_ch1: Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Rv (MTB) was grown from a frozen stock to mid-log phase in 7H9 medium (Becton Dickinson), supplemented with albumin-dextrose-catalase (ADC, Becton Dickinson). Before infection, bacteria were washed three times and re-suspended in 1 ml PBS. Clumps were dissociated by 30 passages through a needle and then allowed to sediment for 5 min. Bacteria in the supernatant were then killed by heating at 90°C during 30min (defined heareafter hk-MTB). The density of bacteria in the supernatant was verified by measuring the OD600 and aliquot volumes defined to allow five bacterium-per two cell infection. Cells were infected in 6-well plates with each well containing 1.3 × 106 cells in 3 ml medium containing GM-CSF (R&D Systems), and treated with 20 µg/mL amikacin (AMK), 20 µg/mL ethambutol (EMB), 10 µg/mL isoniazid (INH), 200 µg/mL pyrazinamide (PZA), or 20 µg/mL rifampicin (RIF) for 18 h.

title: Uninfected_Donor4_PZA_Uninfected
treatment_protocol_ch1: Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Rv (MTB) was grown from a frozen stock to mid-log phase in 7H9 medium (Becton Dickinson), supplemented with albumin-dextrose-catalase (ADC, Becton Dickinson). Before infection, bacteria were washed three times and re-suspended in 1 ml PBS. Clumps were dissociated by 30 passages through a needle and then allowed to sediment for 5 min. Bacteria in the supernatant were then killed by heating at 90°C during 30min (defined heareafter hk-MTB). The density of bacteria in the supernatant was verified by measuring the OD600 and aliquot volumes defined to allow five bacterium-per two cell infection. Cells were infected in 6-well plates with each well containing 1.3 × 106 cells in 3 ml medium containing GM-CSF (R&D Systems), and treated with 20 µg/mL amikacin (AMK), 20 µg/mL ethambutol (EMB), 10 µg/mL isoniazid (INH), 200 µg/mL pyrazinamide (PZA), or 20 µg/mL rifampicin (RIF) for 18 h.
growth_protocol_ch1: Blood mononuclear cells from 4 healthy volunteers (Etablissement Français du Sang, Paris, France) were isolated by Ficoll-Paque (GE Healthcare Life Sciences) centrifugation. CD14+ monocytes were isolated by positive selection using CD14 microbeads (Miltenyi Biotec). Recovered cells were seeded at 2 × 106 cells/well in six-well plates (Falcon; BD Biosciences) in 3 ml RPMI 1640 (Invitrogen) supplemented with 10% heat-inactivated fetal calf serum (Dutscher), L-glutamine, GM-CSF (20 ng/ml; R&D Systems). Cultures were fed every 2 days with complete medium containing full doses of cytokine. Monocytes were allowed to differentiate into macrophages for 6 days at 37°C in 5% CO2 humidified atmosphere.
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted using QIAzol reagent (Life Technologies) and purified over RNeasy columns (Qiagen). The quality of all samples was assessed with an Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies) to verify RNA integrity. Only samples with a good RNA yield and no RNA degradation (ratio of 28S to 18S, > 1.7; RNA integrity number > 9) were used for further experiments.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: cDNA libraries were prepared with the Illumina TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit v2 and were sequenced on an llumina HiSeq 2500
data_processing: STAR v2.5.0b was used to map RNA-seq reads to the hg38 reference genome and quantify  gene expression (option--quantMode GeneCounts) by counting the fragments overlapping the Ensembl genes (GRCh38 v. 83).
data_processing.2: Supplementary_files_format_and_content: The raw counts were generated using STAR v2.5.0b for GRCh38 Ensembl genes
